name.en: White Marble Reclining Buddha (Wat Pa Phu Kon)
name.nl: Witte marmeren liggende Boeddha (Wat Pa Phu Kon)

address: Ban Na Kham Yai, Ban Kong, on the Udon Thani-Loei-Nong Khai border area
opening_hours: Temple access is generally daytime-focused, but current local opening conditions should be rechecked before departure.

description.en: A major Upper Isan temple stop centered on a 20-metre white-marble reclining Buddha, best treated as a deliberate regional detour rather than a quick city add-on.
description.nl: Een grote tempelstop in Upper Isan rond een 20 meter lange witte marmeren liggende Boeddha, het best te zien als bewuste regionale uitstap en niet als snelle stadsuitbreiding.

metaDescription.en: Why the white marble reclining Buddha at Wat Pa Phu Kon is a serious Upper Isan temple detour rather than a casual Nong Khai city stop.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom de witte marmeren liggende Boeddha bij Wat Pa Phu Kon een serieuze Upper Isan-tempeluitstap is en geen losse Nong Khai-stadsstop.

enhanced_description:
This attraction needs a more careful framing than the old index text gave it. TAT's current material places Wat Pa Phu Kon in a wider Upper Isan route and describes the reclining Buddha as a 20-metre figure made of white marble from Carrara in Italy. That is a real, high-value temple detail.

But it is not a casual in-town Nong Khai attraction. It works best for travelers who are already building a larger border-region route and want one serious, visually distinct Buddhist stop.
detailed_history: TAT's current Udon Thani and Nong Khai route article and its province guide to Udon Thani both continue to frame Wat Pa Phu Kon through the white-marble reclining Buddha and the temple's setting across the border area of Udon Thani, Loei, and Nong Khai.
visitor_experience: Expect a dedicated temple detour rather than a quick city errand. The reclining Buddha is the core draw, and the stop makes most sense when your itinerary already extends beyond central Nong Khai.

best_time_to_visit.duration: About 90 to 120 minutes plus transit time

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Use a car or driver on a wider Upper Isan route rather than trying to fit it into a compact Nong Khai city day.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Better paired with a larger regional temple and nature route than with the Nong Khai riverfront alone.
practical_info.food_options: Plan meals around the drive, not around the temple alone.
practical_info.shopping: Minimal; the site is about worship and architecture.

insider_tips: Use this only if you genuinely want a wider regional detour.
Do not present it as a central Nong Khai city attraction.
Dress respectfully and treat it as an active sacred site.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on current TAT route and province material. The site sits better in a wider Upper Isan route than in a compact Nong Khai city walk.
cultural_significance: The temple matters because the reclining Buddha is one of the stronger sacred-art statements in the wider border-region route, even if it is not geographically central to Nong Khai city itself.

contentSources.name: 3D2N to Udon Thani and Nong Khai For Beliefs and Beyond
